sources: Google Cloud Vertex AI — Structured output docs: 'Include your response schema as part of the responseSchema field only. Don't duplicate the schema in your input prompt. If you do, the generated output might be lower in quality.'
Firebase AI Logic — Generate structured output: responseSchema + responseMimeType: 'application/json' is the correct controlled-generation pattern for Gemini
Google Developers Blog — Mastering Controlled Generation with Gemini 1.5: recommends schema-only in responseSchema, not duplicated in prompt
Medium (Polong Lin, Google Cloud): Consistent JSON output via controlled generation requires responseSchema attachment, not prompt-embedded schema
validations.note: Score increase from 1.5 to 3.5 is well-supported. Temperature=0.1 + top_p=0.95 is confirmed best practice for structured JSON (industry consensus: 0.0–0.3 for Gemini 2.5 Pro). responseSchema correctly attached for builders 2 and 3. The held-back ceiling is also justified: Google's own docs explicitly warn against duplicating schema in both prompt and responseSchema — builder 1 (grounded) still carries the embedded schema markdown block alongside the API-level responseSchema on builders 2 and 3, which is the exact anti-pattern the docs warn against. Score of 3.5 rather than 4.0–5.0 is appropriately calibrated.

sources: AWS guidance: Full-jitter exponential backoff — baseline retry pattern; 60–80% reduction in retry storms vs no jitter. Base delay 1–2s, factor 2x, cap ~30–60s.

RFC 7231 (datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7231): Retry-After header has two legal forms — delay-seconds (non-negative integer) AND HTTP-date (e.g., 'Fri, 31 Dec 1999 23:59:59 GMT'). Only numeric form currently handled.
apxml.com — Handling LLM API Errors: distinguish API-failure retries (retry same prompt) vs validation-failure retries (inject error into next prompt) — these are different failure modes.
dev.to — Building a Retry System with Exponential Backoff for LLM API Calls in Python: max 3–7 attempts, permanent error classes (4xx) must not retry, transient (5xx, 429) must use backoff.
Medium (Feb 2026) — Error Handling & Retries: Making LLM Calls Reliable: 429 with Retry-After must be respected; safety/content-filter blocks are non-retryable permanent failures.
validations.note: Score increase from 1.5 to 4.5 is strongly supported. The error hierarchy (GeminiPermanentError/GeminiRateLimitError/GeminiTransientError), full-jitter exponential backoff capped at 30s, max 3 attempts, Retry-After honored, safety blocks treated as permanent — all match authoritative guidance precisely. The two minor gaps the evaluator identifies are also externally confirmed: (1) RFC 7231 explicitly defines two Retry-After formats and handling only numeric-seconds form is incomplete; (2) the apxml.com source directly confirms that API-failure and validation-failure retries are distinct paths that should not be conflated. The 0.5-point deduction from a perfect 5 is appropriate given these confirmed gaps.

sources: redis.io LLMOps Guide 2026: 'Cache at every safe layer — exact-match for identical inputs. Avoid duplicate upstream work; cache by a deterministic key derived from stable inputs.'
AWS — Optimize LLM response costs and latency with effective caching: duplicate fetches and uncached deterministic LLM calls are a primary cost and latency driver in LLM pipelines.

tokenoptimize.dev: 'Token optimization is a context-engineering problem — bloated context, idle tool schemas, and stale history drive cost more than verbose phrasing.' Duplicate fetch_webpage_context is a pure waste.
Google Cloud Vertex AI structured output docs: schema should be in responseSchema parameter only, not embedded in packet markdown — partial progress acknowledged by evaluator (2 of 3 builders fixed).
validations.note: Modest increase from 2.0 to 2.5 is justified. Partial schema attachment to builders 2 and 3 is confirmed progress. The evaluator's ceiling argument is also well-grounded: duplicate webpage fetch remains unaddressed (two full HTTP crawls per end-to-end request), no persona cache exists, and the schema duplication anti-pattern now explicitly applies to 2 of 3 builders. All three of the major unresolved issues are confirmed by external best-practice sources.
